steps.title: Look for emotional attachment
steps.content: The claim says the narrator has a strong emotional attachment to the surrounding forests. The best quotation must show direct personal affection for the woods themselves, not just describe the town, family, scenery, landscape, or forest activities.

steps.title: Separate feeling from setting
steps.content: Choice A focuses on the narrator's parents. Choice C describes the landscape and its poverty. Choice D describes activities under the trees. The quotation that calls the woods favorites and says it makes the narrator's heart bleed to see one cut down shows emotional attachment to the forests. The answer is B.
